Hello CSS Grid Lanes | That HTML Blog","url":"https://thathtml.blog/2026/04/masonry-now-css-grid-lanes/"},{"title":"CSS Grid Lanes: The New Native Masonry Layout","url":"https://blog.openreplay.com/css-grid-lanes-masonry-layout/"},{"title":"CSS Grid Lanes Kills Masonry.js: WebKit Ships Native CSS | byteiota","url":"https://byteiota.com/css-grid-lanes-kills-masonry-js-webkit-ships-native-css/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the CSS masonry specification and CSS Grid Lanes:

## Key Resolution (January 2025)

It was resolved to reuse grid templating and placement properties for masonry layout. This decision came after the CSS Working Group debated for five years (2020-2025) about whether to integrate masonry into CSS Grid or create a separate module.

## Final Syntax: grid-lanes

Setting grid-lanes on the display property activates a masonry layout. Level 3 of the CSS grid layout specification defines masonry layout (also known as grid-lanes layout), which is accessed via the display values grid-lanes and inline-grid-lanes.

## How It Works

CSS Grid Lanes adds a new display mode that creates a masonry-style layout using the familiar grid syntax. Masonry layout is a layout method where one axis uses a typical strict grid layout, most often columns, and the other a stacking (masonry) layout. On the stacking axis, rather than sticking to a strict grid with gaps being left after shorter items, the items in the following row rise up to fill the gaps.

## Browser Support (2025-2026)

In 2026, CSS Grid Lanes (formerly known as the masonry proposal) is the native browser answer — and Safari 26 just shipped it first. As of early 2026, CSS Grid Lanes is available in Safari 26 (first to ship). Chrome and Firefox have the feature behind experimental flags and are expected to ship stable support later in 2026.

## Specification Timeline

The specification published March 28, 2025 as CSS Grid Layout Level 3.
